pattern: A subtraction game where each turn removes one, two, or three stones is a losing position for the mover exactly at multiples of four, so the winner is decided by a single modulo check.
hints: Play out the smallest cases by hand: with one, two, or three stones the mover takes them all and wins; with four the mover always hands the opponent a winning count.
A position is winning if any legal move leads to a losing position for the opponent, and losing if every move hands the opponent a win; tabulate a few and a period of four emerges.
You win precisely when the stone count is not divisible by four, so return whether n mod 4 is nonzero.

edge_inputs.description: four stones is the first losing position
five stones, win by leaving four
eight stones is another losing multiple of four
maximum n, not a multiple of four so a win

stress.description: moderate counts so the O(n) game-theory oracle stays fast while exercising both outcomes
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    return [rng.randrange(1, max(2, size) + 1)]
